name: WMI Process Creation via wmic.exe Command Line
description: Detects wmic.exe invoked with 'process call create' arguments. This is the command-line interface to Win32_Process::Create, a primary WMI execution primitive abused by attackers to spawn processes through WMI, bypassing parent-child chain detection logic that monitors conventional process creation paths.
query: process where process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.args : "process" and process.args : "call" and process.args : "create"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: WMI Provider Host Spawning Unexpected Child Process
description: Detects wmiprvse.exe spawning child processes that are not part of normal WMI administration. When attackers use WMI for code execution, the OS assigns wmiprvse.exe as the parent process — an artifact that exists regardless of whether the attacker used wmic.exe, PowerShell, or a compiled custom tool. Excludes known-legitimate WMI child processes such as mofcomp.exe and unsecapp.exe.
query: process where process.parent.name : "wmiprvse.exe" and not process.name : ("mofcomp.exe", "unsecapp.exe", "scrcons.exe", "wbemtest.exe", "wmiprvse.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: WMI Shadow Copy Enumeration - Pre-Ransomware Indicator
description: Detects wmic.exe querying shadow copy information. Shadow copy enumeration is a standard pre-ransomware reconnaissance step performed by Ryuk, Conti, LockBit, BlackCat, and most other ransomware families prior to backup deletion. Detecting this enumeration provides early warning before the destructive phase.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: WMI Event Subscription Persistence via PowerShell
description: Detects PowerShell creating WMI event subscription persistence objects (__EventFilter, CommandLineEventConsumer, ActiveScriptEventConsumer, __FilterToConsumerBinding). WMI event subscriptions survive reboots, run without any filesystem payload, and are used by APT29, MuddyWater, and many other threat actors for stealthy long-term persistence.
query: process where process.name : "powershell.exe" and (process.command_line : "*__EventFilter*" or process.command_line : "*CommandLineEventConsumer*" or process.command_line : "*ActiveScriptEventConsumer*" or process.command_line : "*__FilterToConsumerBinding*" or process.command_line : "*Register-WmiEvent*" or (process.command_line : "*New-CimInstance*" and process.command_line : "*subscription*"))
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: WMI Execution Chain - Enumeration Followed by Process Creation
description: Sequence detection: identifies hosts where wmic.exe process enumeration is followed within 5 minutes by WMI-based process creation or wmiprvse.exe spawning a child process. This sequential recon-then-execute pattern is strongly indicative of attacker activity rather than legitimate automation, which does not enumerate running processes before executing new ones.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m
  [process where process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*process*list*"]
  [any where
    (process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*process*call*create*")
    or (process.parent.name : "wmiprvse.exe" and not process.name : ("mofcomp.exe", "unsecapp.exe", "scrcons.exe"))
  ]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
